terrain features, structures, and planetary formations blocking movement
visual disappearance of equipped weapon icon/HUD element

automatic acquisition of two maximum CLAWs; support units that mirror player firepower

faction_indicators.color_identity: typically blue/cyan (player ship Styx color scheme)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Federation-aligned spacecraft, clean technological design aesthetic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: heavily mechanized and bio-organic enemy designs across five planets; Cerberus defense system
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: hostile planetary installations, cloaking device infrastructure, ORN headquarters environment

world_state_to_visual.world_state: ORN cloaking devices active (five planets concealed)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: obscured planetary environments with technological defense installations visible; stage design shows protected, fortified structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increased enemy density and aggressive patterns; stage hazards escalate as player progresses through each cloaking device destruction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: large-scale enemy sprites and complex attack patterns during stage progressions; visual indicators of remote defense systems
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: larger enemy waves, complex projectile patterns, stage-wide hazards; difficulty spike reflecting Cerberus efficiency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ORN headquarters environment becomes visible; visual transition from planetary stages to empire core aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemy patterns and stage design shift from planetary defense to headquarters protection; final boss approach indicated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: bio-computer entity visual design; final stage environment reflecting empire command center
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: maximum difficulty enemy behavior; complex boss attack patterns; final mission objective manifestation

environmental_storytelling.example: Five named planets (Hydra, Gorgon, Seiren, Haides, Ellis) each feature distinct visual environments and enemy types reflecting planet-specific ORN installations
ORN Empire environments feature heavy mechanization, bio-organic hybrid designs, and advanced fortification visuals communicating technologically superior but oppressive faction aesthetic
Early stages show planetary surface installations; mid-stages reveal deeper infrastructure; final stages transition to headquarters core, visually narrating player penetration of ORN defenses
Large, detailed enemy sprites showcase ORN military capability and technological sophistication; variety across stages suggests empire-wide military deployment
Intricate, layered backgrounds communicate environmental depth and planetary characteristics; visual richness establishes stakes and scale of conflict

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to directional input; weapon fire synchronized with equipped tool; speed adjustable across four levels
removal of currently equipped weapon (or all weapons on higher difficulty); loss of CLAWs (if active); visual destruction animation; respawn or game over condition
mimic player ship positioning and fire equipped weapon; maximum two CLAWs operational; lost upon ship destruction
increasing density and aggression as stages progress; complex projectile patterns reflecting Cerberus defense system; stage-specific attack formations
visual representation in stage environment; destruction triggers world-state transition toward headquarters infiltration

notes: Thunder Spirits (SNES port of Thunder Force III/AC) communicates faction conflict through visual contrast: Galaxy Federation represented by player's advanced but small Styx craft (blue aesthetic); ORN Empire manifested through dominant red/orange enemy palette, massive mechanized enemy designs, and progressively fortified environments. World-state progression follows three-act structure: (1) five planetary stages with cloaking device destruction, (2) escalating threat from Cerberus system, (3) headquarters infiltration toward bio-computer emperor. Visual manifestation relies heavily on enemy sprite detail, parallax backgrounds, and environmental variation to establish stakes. Danger communicated through enemy density, projectile complexity, and stage hazard escalation. The game removes level selection (vs. Thunder Force III), suggesting linear narrative progression toward defined climax. Weapon system and CLAW mechanics create resource-scarcity tension: loss of equipped weapon on destruction creates tactical pressure and reinforces vulnerability despite Styx's advanced design.
